SEARCH
Package neqsim.process.equipment.powergeneration.gasturbine

Class GasTurbineUnit

java.lang.Object
neqsim.util.NamedBaseClass
neqsim.process.SimulationBaseClass
neqsim.process.equipment.ProcessEquipmentBaseClass
neqsim.process.equipment.TwoPortEquipment
neqsim.process.equipment.powergeneration.gasturbine.GasTurbineUnit
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable, Runnable, ProcessEquipmentInterface, TwoPortInterface, ProcessElementInterface, SimulationInterface, NamedInterface
public class GasTurbineUnit extends TwoPortEquipment
Catalogue-driven gas turbine unit used for dispatch, right-sizing and lifecycle CO2 / fuel studies.

Unlike GasTurbine (which builds a full Brayton-cycle air/combustion/expansion thermo model), this class is an engineering-level energy accounting wrapper: it takes a fuel gas inlet stream and a list of Compressor power consumers (or arbitrary PowerDemandConsumers), computes load fraction against the supplied GasTurbineSpec, looks up effective heat rate from a GasTurbinePerformanceMap, applies optional GasTurbineDegradation penalties, sizes the fuel gas mass flow, and reports CO2 / NOx / methane-slip emissions via GasTurbineEmissions.

Typical use in a ProcessSystem: 


 GasTurbineUnit gt = new GasTurbineUnit("GT-A", fuelGasStream, GasTurbineCatalog.get("LM2500"));
 gt.addPowerConsumer(exportCompressor);
 gt.addPowerConsumer(injectionCompressor);
 process.add(gt);
 process.run();
 double fuelFlowKgPerHr = gt.getFuelMassFlowKgPerHr();
 double co2KgPerHr = gt.getCO2EmissionKgPerHr();
Version:
$Id: $Id
Author:
neqsim

  • Constructor Details

    • GasTurbineUnit

      public GasTurbineUnit(String name)
      Construct a unit with a name only — fuel stream and spec must be set separately before running.
      Parameters:
      name - unit name

    • GasTurbineUnit

      public GasTurbineUnit(String name, StreamInterface fuelStream, GasTurbineSpec spec)
      Construct a unit with an inlet fuel stream and a catalog spec.
      Parameters:
      name - unit name
      fuelStream - fuel gas inlet stream
      spec - catalog spec (use GasTurbineCatalog.get(String))

  • Method Details

    • setSpec

      public final void setSpec(GasTurbineSpec spec)
      Set the catalog spec. Re-initialises the performance map and degradation model from the spec defaults if they have not been overridden.
      Parameters:
      spec - catalog spec

    • setPerformanceMap

      public void setPerformanceMap(GasTurbinePerformanceMap performanceMap)
      Set the performance map (override the type-default map from GasTurbinePerformanceMap.fromSpec(GasTurbineSpec)).
      Parameters:
      performanceMap - performance map

    • setDegradation

      public void setDegradation(GasTurbineDegradation degradation)
      Set the degradation model.
      Parameters:
      degradation - degradation model

    • setEmissions

      public void setEmissions(GasTurbineEmissions emissions)
      Set the emissions model (override the default no-slip model).
      Parameters:
      emissions - emissions model

    • setAmbient

      public void setAmbient(double temperatureK, double pressureBara)
      Set site ambient conditions for ISO power / heat-rate correction.
      Parameters:
      temperatureK - ambient temperature [K]
      pressureBara - ambient pressure [bara]

    • setAmbientTemperatureK

      public void setAmbientTemperatureK(double temperatureK)
      Convenience setter for ambient temperature only. Leaves ambient pressure at its current value (defaults to ISO sea-level, 1.01325 bara).
      Parameters:
      temperatureK - ambient temperature [K]

    • setAmbientPressureBara

      public void setAmbientPressureBara(double pressureBara)
      Convenience setter for ambient pressure only. Leaves ambient temperature unchanged (defaults to ISO 15 °C = 288.15 K).
      Parameters:
      pressureBara - ambient pressure [bara]

    • addPowerConsumer

      public void addPowerConsumer(Compressor compressor)
      Add a compressor as a power consumer.
      Parameters:
      compressor - compressor whose getPower() will be summed into demand

    • addPowerConsumer

      public void addPowerConsumer(PowerDemandConsumer consumer)
      Add a generic power consumer.
      Parameters:
      consumer - consumer implementation

      public void clearPowerConsumers()
      Remove all attached consumers.

    • setDemandedPower

      public void setDemandedPower(double powerW)
      Set an explicit demanded power, overriding any attached consumers.
      Parameters:
      powerW - demanded power [W]

    • clearDemandedPowerOverride

      public void clearDemandedPowerOverride()
      Clear the explicit demand override and revert to summing consumers.

    • run

      public void run(UUID id)

      In this method all thermodynamic and unit operations will be calculated in a steady state calculation.

      id - UUID

      public double getLoadFraction()
      Get the current load fraction.
      Returns:
      load fraction (1.0 = at site rated)

      public double getEffectiveHeatRateKJPerKWh()
      Get the effective heat rate (LHV basis) at the current operating point.
      Returns:
      heat rate [kJ/kWh]

      public double getThermalEfficiency()
      Get the current thermal efficiency.

      thermal efficiency (0–1)

      Get the exhaust temperature.
      Returns:
      exhaust T [K]

    • isOverloaded

      public boolean isOverloaded()
      Indicate whether the demand exceeded the available power on the last run.
      Returns:
      true if overloaded

    • isBelowMinLoad

      public boolean isBelowMinLoad()
      Indicate whether the load fell below the minimum stable load fraction.
      Returns:
      true if below minimum stable load

      public List<PowerDemandConsumer> getPowerConsumers()
      Get the list of attached power consumers.

      defensive copy of the consumer list
